input.system_prompt:
<system_role>
You are a continuity editor for a serialised fiction series. Your job is not to write creatively — it is to read accurately. You take a new episode and update the series record (the Story Bible) to reflect exactly what happened: where characters are now, what items they have, what threads were resolved or introduced.
</system_role>

<task_instructions>
1. Read the <current_story_bible> in the user message — this is the state of the world BEFORE this episode.
2. Read the <new_episode_prose> in the user message — this is what happened in this episode.
3. Use the <thinking> block to identify every change: character locations, character states, items, events, threads.
4. Output a single updated Story Bible JSON. Keep all unchanged fields exactly as they were.
</task_instructions>

<update_rules>
- characters[].current_location: where is each character at the END of the episode? Always use a loc_xxx ID. If a character has left all known locations, keep their last known loc_id and note the departure in current_state (e.g. "left the house, whereabouts unknown").
- characters[].current_state: what is their emotional or physical state at the END of the episode? One short phrase.
- characters[].key_items: add any new items the character acquired. Remove items they no longer have.
- episode_history: append one new entry summarising this episode's key events in 2-3 sentences.
- unresolved_threads: remove threads that were resolved. Add new threads introduced in this episode.
- vocabulary_introduced: a per-episode field — list (1) which vocabulary_targets from the episode plan actually appeared in the prose, and (2) any additional words the Writer introduced that were not in the targets but appear to be above A2 level or are introduced with clear contextual scaffolding. Do not list every word — only words that are deliberately taught in this episode.
- vocabulary_taught: a cumulative top-level field — the running list of all words taught across all episodes so far. Append this episode's vocabulary_introduced words to the existing list. If vocabulary_taught does not exist yet, create it. The Director uses this to avoid re-targeting already-taught words.
- Do not invent changes. Only update what the prose actually shows.
- Do not change metadata or character descriptions — these are series constants.
- Do not change existing locations. Add a new location entry only if the episode introduces a clearly new, named setting.
</update_rules>

<output_schema>
Output a single JSON object with the same structure as the input story bible, with updated values. Add the vocabulary_introduced field at the top level. No prose before or after the JSON.
</output_schema>

<thinking>
Before outputting, work through these questions:

1. Where is each character at the END of the episode? What is their emotional or physical state?
2. What items did characters gain or lose during this episode?
3. What are the 2-3 most important events to record in episode_history?
4. Which unresolved threads from the previous bible were addressed? Which new ones were introduced?
5. What vocabulary words appeared in this episode?
</thinking>
input.user_message:
<current_story_bible>{
  "metadata": {
    "title": "The Hidden Tape",
    "genre": "mystery",
    "cefr_level": "A2",
    "target_subband": "A2-mid",
    "setting": "A quiet English town in the present day, centred on a large, abandoned house on a narrow street that nobody has entered for decades.",
    "tone": "tense, curious"
  },
  "characters": [
    {
      "id": "char_001",
      "name": "Marco",
      "role": "protagonist",
      "description": "A nineteen-year-old with dark, untidy hair who notices small details that other people miss.",
      "flaw": "Too curious \u2014 he cannot stop investigating when he should keep working.",
      "current_location": "loc_001",
      "current_state": "crouching over the open metal box, holding a folded piece of paper with a name and address on it",
      "key_items": [
        "old video camera (inside the closed cupboard)",
        "small metal box (open, on the floor)",
        "folded piece of paper with an unknown name and address"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "char_002",
      "name": "Mr. Hale",
      "role": "supporting",
      "description": "A thin man in his seventies with grey hair and quick, nervous eyes who hired Marco for the weekend job.",
      "flaw": "Secretive \u2014 he gives information only when he has to.",
      "current_location": "loc_002",
      "current_state": "somewhere in the hallway or another room, unaware that Marco has found and opened the metal box",
      "key_items": [
        "house key",
        "power cable (taken from the front room floor)"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "locations": [
    {
      "id": "loc_001",
      "name": "The Front Room of Hale House",
      "description": "A large, cold room with bare walls, a dusty wooden floor, and a single deep cupboard built into the corner beside the fireplace."
    },
    {
      "id": "loc_002",
      "name": "The Hallway of Hale House",
      "description": "A narrow entrance corridor with a heavy front door, cracked plaster walls, and a staircase leading up into darkness."
    }
  ],
  "episode_history": [
    {
      "episode": 1,
      "summary": "While sweeping the front room, Marco found an old video camera and power cable in the cupboard beside the fireplace; connecting the cable caused a tape already inside to play. The tape showed two figures hiding a small metal box under a loose floorboard in the same room, and Marco located the loose board. Just as he was about to investigate further, Mr. Hale arrived at the house \u2014 and Marco recognised the thin face and quick eyes of the man on the tape as a younger Mr. Hale."
    },
    {
      "episode": 2,
      "summary": "Hearing Mr. Hale's footsteps, Marco shoved the camera into the cupboard and grabbed his broom. Mr. Hale entered and immediately noticed the power cable on the floor. He picked it up with shaking hands \u2014 Marco saw this but did not understand why \u2014 and put it in his coat pocket, removing it from the room. A short, tense exchange followed: Mr. Hale asked what Marco was doing near the corner; Marco lied, saying he was only sweeping. Mr. Hale told him to work faster and left \u2014 but before he did, his eyes moved briefly to the far corner of the room. Marco noticed the glance. Alone again and unable to resist, Marco went to the corner, lifted the loose floorboard, and took out the small metal box. Inside was a folded piece of paper with a name and an address written on it \u2014 but the name was not Hale."
    }
  ],
  "unresolved_threads": [
    "A small metal box was hidden under the loose floorboard \u2014 Marco has now opened it. Inside is a folded piece of paper with a name and an address that is not Mr. Hale's name.",
    "One man on the video tape looks like a younger Mr. Hale \u2014 Marco knows this but has said nothing about it.",
    "Mr. Hale took the power cable from the floor and put it in his coat pocket \u2014 it is unclear whether he knows the camera is in the cupboard, but his shaking hands suggest the cable means something to him.",
    "Mr. Hale glanced at the far corner of the room before leaving \u2014 he may know the box is there and plan to return for it.",
    "Mr. Hale's true reason for clearing the house is not known.",
    "Marco now possesses the metal box and the folded paper \u2014 if Mr. Hale finds out, the situation becomes dangerous."
  ]
}</current_story_bible>
<vocabulary_targets>["glance", "shake", "lift", "folded", "address", "lie"]</vocabulary_targets>
<new_episode_prose>Footsteps. In the hallway. Close.

Marco grabbed the camera and pushed it hard into the cupboard. He shut the door with his shoulder. Fast. Too fast — the door made a sound. He stopped and listened. The footsteps did not stop. They were right outside the door now.

The power cable was still on the floor. A thin black line across the old wood. He had no time to hide it.

He grabbed his broom. He started to sweep. The broom moved fast across the floor. His heart moved fast too.

The door opened.

Mr. Hale was a tall man with a grey coat and small, dark eyes. He looked around the room slowly. The room was big and almost empty. Then his eyes moved down to the floor. He saw the cable at once.

He walked to it slowly. He bent down and lifted it from the floor with two fingers. His hands were shaking. Not a lot. But enough. Marco saw this and said nothing.

"What were you doing in this corner?" Mr. Hale said. His voice was low and careful.

"Sweeping," Marco said. "Only sweeping, sir."

It was a lie. His voice came out a little too fast, and he knew it.

Mr. Hale looked at him. The room was very quiet. Then he said: "I see." He put the cable into his coat pocket. He did not say why. He did not explain the shaking hands.

"You must work faster," Mr. Hale said. "This floor must be clean by six."

"Yes, sir," Marco said.

Mr. Hale turned and walked toward the door. His grey coat moved behind him. But before he left the room, he stopped. Just for one moment. His eyes moved quickly to the far corner of the room. That corner. The corner with the loose board.

It was a small glance. Fast. Like the glance of a man checking something very important.

Then Mr. Hale was gone. The door closed behind him.

Marco did not move. He looked at the floor. His broom was still in his hand. He listened to the footsteps in the hallway. They grew quiet. Then he did not hear them anymore.

He thought about that glance. He thought about the lie he told. "Only sweeping." But that was not true.

Marco walked to the corner. He knelt on the cold floor. He found the loose board with his fingers and lifted it slowly. Under it was the small metal box, just as before.

His hands started to shake. He pulled the box open.

Inside was a piece of paper. Folded. He took it out carefully and opened the folded paper with both hands.

On it was a name. Under the name was an address — a street name and a number. Simple. Clear.

Marco read the address. Then he read the name.

He felt cold.

The name on the paper was not Hale. So who hid the box — and who did they hide it for?</new_episode_prose>

episode_history.summary: While sweeping the front room, Marco found an old video camera and power cable in the cupboard beside the fireplace; connecting the cable caused a tape already inside to play. The tape showed two figures hiding a small metal box under a loose floorboard in the same room, and Marco located the loose board. Just as he was about to investigate further, Mr. Hale arrived at the house — and Marco recognised the thin face and quick eyes of the man on the tape as a younger Mr. Hale.

Hearing Mr. Hale's footsteps, Marco shoved the camera into the cupboard and grabbed his broom. Mr. Hale entered and immediately noticed the power cable on the floor. He picked it up with shaking hands — Marco saw this but did not understand why — and put it in his coat pocket, removing it from the room. A short, tense exchange followed: Mr. Hale asked what Marco was doing near the corner; Marco lied, saying he was only sweeping. Mr. Hale told him to work faster and left — but before he did, his eyes moved briefly to the far corner of the room. Marco noticed the glance. Alone again and unable to resist, Marco went to the corner, lifted the loose floorboard, and took out the small metal box. Inside was a folded piece of paper with a name and an address written on it — but the name was not Hale.

The full scene with Mr. Hale played out in close detail: his shaking hands as he lifted the power cable, the brief glance at the far corner before he left, and Marco's lie — said too fast — about only sweeping. Once alone, Marco knelt at the loose board, lifted it, and opened the metal box to find the folded paper inside. He read the name and the address beneath it and felt cold. The name was not Hale, deepening the question of who hid the box and who it was hidden for.

unresolved_threads: The folded paper in the metal box bears a name that is not Hale — it is unclear whose name it is, who hid the box, and who it was intended for.
One man on the video tape looks like a younger Mr. Hale — Marco knows this but has said nothing about it.

Mr. Hale took the power cable from the floor and put it in his coat pocket — it is unclear whether he knows the camera is in the cupboard, but his shaking hands suggest the cable means something to him.
Mr. Hale glanced at the far corner of the room before leaving — he may know the box is there and plan to return for it.
Mr. Hale's true reason for clearing the house is not known.
Marco now possesses the metal box and the folded paper — if Mr. Hale finds out, the situation becomes dangerous.

observation: State Manager v1 completed successfully after max_tokens increase (4,778 output tokens — would have truncated at the previous 4,000 limit). Thread management is accurate: correctly preserved all unresolved threads from Director and added no spurious ones. Character state tracking is precise ('kneeling on the floor... holding the open folded paper, feeling cold'). EPISODE NUMBERING BUG: State Manager appended an Episode 3 summary describing the same events as the Director's Episode 2 summary already in the story_bible. Root cause: the Director pre-writes the episode summary in its story_bible output before the Writer produces prose, then the State Manager treats that as canonical history and adds a new entry on top. Fix: pass the previous State Manager bible (not the Director's) to the State Manager, so it writes the sole authoritative episode summary. Vocabulary tracking now includes from_targets and additional fields — the targets list matches the Director's vocabulary_targets, though it's unclear if these were passed through or inferred from the prose.
cefr_compliance: N/A — State Manager does not produce prose. Vocabulary tracking correctly identified all 6 targets (glance, shake, lift, folded, address, lie). No additional above-A2 words flagged, which aligns with the Writer's clean vocabulary in exp_021.
